title: Who Shot Mr Burns 2

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature exclamation as the episode opens
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase that signals we're in for classic Homer antics
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration and economy, but it's just a catchphrase without context here

jokes.text: Ick, my mouth tastes like an ashtray.
jokes.setup: Barney waking up after drinking
jokes.punchline: The disgusting but relatable morning-after sensation
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment for Barney but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Sir, you weren't shot. It was all a dream. That's right. The year is 1965. And you and I are undercover detectives on the hot-rod circuit. Now let's burn rubber, baby!
jokes.setup: Burns seems confused about reality after being shot
jokes.punchline: The elaborate 1960s cop show fantasy with Burns as a hot-rod detective
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist moment that plays well with Burns's character while subverting expectations

jokes.text: Speedway Squad! In color.
jokes.setup: Following the 1960s detective fantasy
jokes.punchline: The period-appropriate TV show title card with 'in color' emphasis
jokes.explanation: Nice period detail that enhances the fantasy sequence

jokes.text: Wait. That was all a dream. Aw! Hey, then maybe I haven't become a hideous, drunken wreck.
jokes.setup: Barney realizes his pleasant dream wasn't real
jokes.punchline: His hope that maybe his alcoholism was also just a dream
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - self-aware about his condition with dark humor

jokes.text: Burns was rushed to a nearby hospital, where he was pronounced dead. He was then transferred to a better hospital, where doctors upgraded his condition to alive.
jokes.setup: News report about Burns's medical treatment
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of being 'upgraded' from dead to alive like a flight status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect deadpan delivery and medical bureaucracy satire

jokes.text: Oh, it's no use. They ain't talkin'.
jokes.setup: Wiggum questioning witnesses
jokes.punchline: The 'witnesses' are clearly babies who literally can't talk
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence with a clever visual element

jokes.text: Kids, kids, kids. As far as Daddy's concerned, you're both potential murderers.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa arguing about who could have shot Burns
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance that both his children are capable of murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting logic with dark humor that's both shocking and in character

jokes.text: Oh, I bet he's the one. Yeah, Smingers did it. Case closed. Now, where's my hat? I'm going to the outhouse.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Smithers being a suspect
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's mispronunciation and immediate topic change to primitive facilities
jokes.explanation: Good Grandpa characterization with the name confusion and outdated references

jokes.text: We don't have an outhouse. - My toolshed! Oh, Dad!
jokes.setup: Grandpa looking for an outhouse
jokes.punchline: His backup plan is equally inappropriate and Marge's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up to the outhouse joke with family dynamics

jokes.text: Sure. I've had it up to here with these damn rickets.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to tear down Burns's sun-blocking machine
jokes.punchline: His complaint about rickets, a Victorian-era disease from lack of sunlight
jokes.explanation: Great Homer ignorance moment - using an archaic disease term seriously

jokes.text: Um, uh, what town did we just crush? - Shelbyville.
jokes.setup: The machine being pulled down
jokes.punchline: They accidentally destroyed the wrong town entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and absurdist payoff to the destruction sequence

jokes.text: Father, I'm not a Catholic, but- Well, I tried to march in the St. Patrick's Day parade. Anyway, I've got a rather large sin to confess.
jokes.setup: Homer seeking confession despite not being Catholic
jokes.punchline: His qualification is marching in a parade, not actual religious practice
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic about religious qualification through cultural participation

jokes.text: That's all I needed to hear. Boy, this thing works great.
jokes.setup: Homer confessing to shooting Burns
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's casual reaction and focus on his recording device working
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of confession sanctity with Lovejoy's technology enthusiasm

jokes.text: This isn't a rival company you're battling with. It's a school. People won't stand for it. - Pish-posh. It will be like taking candy from a baby. Say, that sounds like a 'larf.'
jokes.setup: Smithers warning Burns about public reaction
jokes.punchline: Burns dismissing concern and being amused by his own cliché
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that sets up a later payoff about literally taking candy

jokes.text: Bah! He was a rank amateur... compared to Dr. Colossus.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Burns as a super-villain
jokes.punchline: Random criminal in jail casually referencing a comic book villain name
jokes.explanation: Fun absurdist moment with the comic book villain reference

jokes.text: Kent, I- I feel about as low as Madonna... when she found out she missed Tailhook. - I'm gonna say ouch for Madonna.
jokes.setup: Smithers describing his emotional state
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison to Madonna missing a military scandal
jokes.explanation: Weak topical reference that doesn't land well and feels forced

jokes.text: Hey, that's my Madonna gag. That guy stole my gag. - And you stole it from last Friday's episode of Pardon My Zinger.
jokes.setup: Kent claiming his joke was stolen
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he stole it too from a TV show
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about joke theft and media recycling content

jokes.text: Stole, made up-what's the difference?
jokes.setup: Being caught stealing jokes
jokes.punchline: Kent's casual dismissal of intellectual property
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kent's media cynicism

jokes.text: Really? Oh, geez. All right, Colossus, you're free to go. - But stay away from Death Mountain. - But all my stuff is there.
jokes.setup: Wiggum releasing the wrong prisoner
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to comic book villain locations and belongings
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist follow-through with Wiggum's incompetence and comic book logic

jokes.text: Slow down. The sidewalk's for regular walkin', not for fancy walkin'.
jokes.setup: Smithers hurrying past Grandpa
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's arbitrary distinction between walking speeds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa logic - creating arbitrary rules about normal activities

jokes.text: Wait a minute. If a second old geezer got shot, how come nobody reported it?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jasper potentially being shot
jokes.punchline: The dark implication that shooting old people isn't newsworthy in Springfield
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about Springfield's violence problem and media priorities

jokes.text: Yeah, it's real spruce. You like it?
jokes.setup: Asked to show his leg
jokes.punchline: Proud of his wooden leg using wood terminology
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay connecting wood type to wooden leg with character pride

jokes.text: You shot who in the what now?
jokes.setup: Smithers apologizing for shooting his wooden leg
jokes.punchline: Jasper's confused reaction to the confession
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat with good phrasing for confusion

jokes.text: Actually, Mom, in 95% of cases, it is. The rest of the time it's usually some deranged lunatic who did it for no reason.
jokes.setup: Marge saying it's never the most likely suspect
jokes.punchline: Lisa's statistical correction about detective fiction vs reality
jokes.explanation: Great Lisa moment - using logic to debunk mystery tropes

jokes.text: Hey, I had a damn good reason. He could never remember my name. Who the devil are you?
jokes.setup: Homer defending his motive for attempted murder
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately proving Homer's point by not remembering him
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - Homer states his motive and Burns immediately validates it

jokes.text: It's Homer Simpson! My name is Homer Simpson!
jokes.setup: Burns asking who Homer is
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated repetitive declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's frustration with the name issue

jokes.text: I never thought I could shoot down a German plane... but last year I proved myself wrong.
jokes.setup: Grandpa saying you never know what you're capable of
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of shooting down a plane in modern times
jokes.explanation: Excellent Grandpa moment - mixing WWII stories with impossible modern scenarios

jokes.text: Why don't you try to solve the mystery of who put that mud in the freezer?
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to help solve the mystery
jokes.punchline: Marge redirecting to a mundane household mystery
jokes.explanation: Good parental deflection with a domestic mystery twist

jokes.text: Who wants chocolate ice cream? - Me! Me! Me, me!
jokes.setup: Marge offering ice cream after mentioning mud in freezer
jokes.punchline: The 'chocolate' ice cream is obviously the mud from the freezer
jokes.explanation: Clever immediate payoff to the freezer mystery with visual disgust humor

jokes.text: Now, let's discuss the, um, 'mo-tive.'
jokes.setup: Wiggum leading the investigation
jokes.punchline: His awkward pronunciation of 'motive' like it's sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum trying to sound professional but revealing his ignorance

jokes.text: That's a real good way to get sick.
jokes.setup: Description of Barney getting coins from a love tester machine
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's obvious hygiene observation delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Mild Wiggum moment with obvious health advice

jokes.text: Let's roll, boys.
jokes.setup: Decision to investigate celebrity Tito Puente
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's action movie enthusiasm for questioning a jazz musician
jokes.explanation: Wiggum treating routine questioning like a major operation

jokes.text: Revenge? Of course. But why wound his body with bullets... when I could set his soul afire with a slanderous mambo?
jokes.setup: Police suspecting Tito of violent revenge
jokes.punchline: His preference for musical revenge over violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing artistic temperament and creative revenge concept

jokes.text: You rotten rich old wretch
jokes.setup: The mambo continuing
jokes.punchline: Alliterative insult in English
jokes.explanation: Good alliteration and escalation in the musical insult sequence

jokes.text: Now I did- I did go to the town meeting with the intention of ambushing Mr. Burns.
jokes.setup: Skinner being questioned about his whereabouts
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of planning an ambush
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's military planning applied to civilian situations

jokes.text: Blast! I took mother's makeup kit by mistake.
jokes.setup: Skinner applying camouflage makeup
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using his mother's cosmetics instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - military precision undermined by domestic confusion

jokes.text: Oh, yes. But anything else he tells you is a filthy lie.
jokes.setup: Confirming Chalmers can vouch for him
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining his credibility preemptively
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner paranoia and relationship dynamic with Chalmers

jokes.text: Look at 'em! Oh, I got it from Space Invaders in 1977.
jokes.setup: Willie's crippling arthritis preventing gun use
jokes.punchline: The arthritis came from playing a video game
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor connecting serious disability to retro gaming

jokes.text: Good, 'cause I got a hot date tonight. A date. Dinner with friends. Dinner alone. Watching TV alone. All right! I'm gonna sit at home and ogle the ladies... in the Victoria Secret Catalog. Sears Catalog.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming to have a hot date
jokes.punchline: The rapid descent from hot date to catalog ogling, then downgrading the catalog
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Moe's pathetic honesty with perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: You accuse me of everything around here. 'Who put slippers in the dishwasher?' 'Who threw a cane at the TV?' 'Who fell into the china hutch?'
jokes.setup: Grandpa being defensive about being questioned
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd list of things he's been accused of (and probably did)
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific escalation showing Grandpa's household chaos

jokes.text: We're out of coffee. Well, I'll just drink this warm cream.
jokes.setup: Wiggum needing coffee for an all-night investigation
jokes.punchline: His solution of drinking warm cream as a substitute
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum logic failure with disgusting but logical alternative

jokes.text: Did you have the same backwards-talking dream with the flaming cards?
jokes.setup: Lou having an idea about checking Burns's suit
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's reference to Twin Peaks supernatural elements
jokes.explanation: Good Twin Peaks reference that fits the mystery genre parody

jokes.text: Did I say weeks? 'Cause I meant seconds.
jokes.setup: DNA test taking 8-10 weeks
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction to seconds for plot convenience
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about TV show pacing versus reality

jokes.text: Yuh-huh. If you've ever handled a penny, the government's got your D.N.A. Why do you think they keep them in circulation?
jokes.setup: Having everyone's DNA on file
jokes.punchline: Conspiracy theory about penny-based DNA collection
jokes.explanation: Creative conspiracy theory humor with economic logic

jokes.text: The gunman has a name-o.
jokes.setup: Discovering the shooter's identity
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's childish 'bingo' reference for dramatic reveal
jokes.explanation: Typical Wiggum inappropriate tone for serious police work

jokes.text: The wagon's too tall. Oh, I don't wanna have to get out. I'll just drive up on the curb.
jokes.setup: Police wagon too tall for drive-through
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's lazy solution causing property damage for convenience
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum incompetence with realistic police procedure failure

jokes.text: Hi, everybody! - Ho... mer Simp... son.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert's cheerful greeting to Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns's slow, confused repetition of Homer's name
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Burns's condition with his fixation on Homer's name

jokes.text: No, no, no, when I took your father's name... I took everything that came with it, including D.N.A.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Marge doesn't have Simpson DNA
jokes.punchline: Marge's misunderstanding of how marriage and genetics work
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge logic failure about marriage and biology

jokes.text: That's right. We all gotta stick together... if we're gonna have any hope of bringing that awful Homer to justice.
jokes.setup: Family needing to stick together during crisis
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's immediate betrayal of his own son
jokes.explanation: Perfect character betrayal with Grandpa's lack of family loyalty

jokes.text: Dead or alive. - Holy cow! Me first! Me first!
jokes.setup: $50,000 reward for Homer's capture
jokes.punchline: Crowd's enthusiastic response to bounty hunting their neighbor
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about community loyalty versus money

jokes.text: We cannot claim the reward unless we have 51% of the carcass.
jokes.setup: Police pursuing Homer for the bounty
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's corporate-style percentage requirement for dead body claims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor mixing corporate logic with bounty hunting

jokes.text: He's innocent. He wouldn't hurt a fly.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending her father
jokes.punchline: Using the cliché phrase about someone clearly capable of violence
jokes.explanation: Weak irony about Homer's obvious capacity for violence

jokes.text: Stop telling them it was me! I'll kill you for saying it was me!
jokes.setup: Homer denying guilt while being violent
jokes.punchline: Threatening murder to prove he's not a murderer
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic escalation - proving Lisa wrong about his peaceful nature immediately

jokes.text: Your kind has neither the cranial capacity... nor the opposable digits to operate a firearm.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why Homer couldn't have shot him
jokes.punchline: Treating Homer like a primitive species scientifically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns condescension using scientific terminology to insult Homer

jokes.text: M-Maggie Simpson!
jokes.setup: Building suspense about who really shot Burns
jokes.punchline: The shooter was a baby
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection reveal - the ultimate innocent perpetrator in a murder mystery

jokes.text: But with him out of the picture, I was free to wallow in my own crapulence.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his freedom after firing Smithers
jokes.punchline: Using 'crapulence' (excess in eating/drinking) pretentiously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns vocabulary - using obscure words to sound sophisticated about base desires

jokes.text: But the old axiom was misleading. Taking the candy proved exceedingly difficult.
jokes.setup: Burns attempting to take candy from baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: The supposedly easy task being actually impossible
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of the cliché showing it's literally not easy

jokes.text: Those paramedics have sticky fingers.
jokes.setup: Burns swallowing his gold fillings
jokes.punchline: Paranoid assumption about medical professionals stealing from patients
jokes.explanation: Good Burns paranoia and mistrust of working-class people

jokes.text: No jury in the world's gonna convict a baby. Hmm, maybe Texas.
jokes.setup: Burns demanding baby Maggie be arrested
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's exception for Texas justice system
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about regional justice stereotypes

tmdb_overview: With Mr. Burns shot, Chief Wiggum investigates the crime, and nearly everyone in town has a motive. At the end, it all comes down to the Simpson DNA.
insight: Resolution pays off the cliffhanger, but 72.5 score marks the series' first notable dip.
